_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: CA federal House batch: mark-desaulnier (CA-10 D incumbent, evidence-scored from 119th roll calls + Heritage 0% + NRA F + heterodox Israel/Iran/Pentagon-waste catches), connie-chan-ca-11 (still-running Pelosi-seat D candidate, advanced to Nov general; deep cited record from SF BoS + campaign platform), and THREE withdrawn CA-11 challengers (cole-bettles, daniel-wheeler, darren-helton) flipped to status:lost + candidacy_status:not_running per CA SoS official ballot. mike-thompson skipped: 3-way slug collision (CA fed + MS state + KS state).

profile.religion: Catholic-raised (Jesuit-educated); Catholic League lists him among 'pro-abortion Catholic politicians.'
profile.background: US Representative CA-10 since 2015 (CA-11 redistricted). Ranking Member, House Ethics Committee (Feb 2025). Former CA State Senator; switched from R to D in 2000.

mark-desaulnier.notes_set: US Rep CA-10 (D, since 2015). [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Bay Area progressive: NAY on HR 21 Born-Alive (Roll 27, 2025), NAY on HR 28 Protection of Women & Girls in Sports (Roll 12), YEA on HR 8404 Respect for Marriage (2022), cosponsor of HR 15 Equality Act (119th), NAY on HR 29/S 5 Laken Riley Act, NAY on HR 22 SAVE Act, NAY on OBBBA reconciliation. Self-identifies as NRA F-rated 'gun violence prevention' advocate. Heritage Action 119th 0% (lifetime 8%); SBA Pro-Life F; TPAction 17.59. HETERODOX-D CATCHES: (1) NAY on HR 8034 Israel $17B supplemental (Apr 2024, Roll 152) — one of 37 D crossovers, publicly thanked by CAIR-CA; (2) YEA on Iran War Powers Resolution + cosponsor H.Con.Res.38 (forces removal from Iran hostilities); (3) introduced DoD Waste Reduction Act (HR 2367) targeting $125B Pentagon waste; (4) NAY on Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023 (one of 46 D left-flank crossovers); (5) not listed on TrackAIPAC — no AIPAC PAC support consistent with his Israel-aid NAY votes.

0.note: Voted NAY on HR 21 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 27, Jan 23 2025); NRLC rating 0; SBA Pro-Life F.

1.note: Cosponsor of Women's Health Protection Act (codifies Roe); called Dobbs 'an assault on the freedom of all women.'

4.note: No itemized PAC-source breakdown obtained; opted not to score without direct evidence of PP/NARAL/EMILY's List donations.

0.note: Voted YEA on HR 8404 Respect for Marriage Act (Roll 373, Dec 8 2022) — codified same-sex marriage federally.

1.note: YEA on Respect for Marriage Act = supports same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Voted NAY on HR 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, Jan 14 2025) — opposed barring trans-identified males from women's sports.

4.note: Cosponsor of HR 15 Equality Act (119th) — promotes LGBTQ identity in federal policy/schools/business; member of Congressional Equality Caucus.

2.note: TPAction American Culture 21.76% / Education Freedom 0.00% — consistent record opposing parental-rights/curriculum-control bills.

1.note: Cosponsor of HR 15 Equality Act, which explicitly overrides RFRA conscience protections for religious medical/business/adoption providers.

2.note: Heritage Action 119th 0% / lifetime 8%; voted NO on Trump OBBBA reconciliation (Roll 190, July 2025) opposing spending cuts. Note: also voted NAY on the Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 — but as a left-flank rejection of the cuts, not fiscal restraint.

4.note: Introduced DoD Waste Reduction Act (HR 2367) targeting $125B Pentagon waste — substantive challenge to defense-contractor capture (cross-aisle ally on Pentagon-waste reform).

1.note: Voted NAY on HR 22 SAVE Act (Roll 102, Apr 10 2025) — opposed documentary proof of citizenship for federal voter registration.
Voted NAY on HR 29 Laken Riley Act (Roll 6) and S 5 (Roll 23) — opposed mandatory detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violence.

2.note: Public condemnation of June 2025 ICE raids in Contra Costa as 'cruel, unnecessary'; demanded answers from Acting ICE Director — defends sanctuary posture.

0.note: Self-identifies as 'proud recipient of an F rating from the NRA'; TPAction 2A score 0.00%.

1.note: Authored multiple gun-control bills (research mandates, smart guns, gun violence prevention); voted YEA Bipartisan Background Checks Act + Bipartisan Safer Communities Act.

0.note: HETERODOX-D: voted YEA on Iran War Powers Resolution (failed 212-219); cosponsor of H.Con.Res.38 directing removal of US forces from Iran hostilities — affirms Article I war-powers requirement.

2.note: HETERODOX-D: NAY on HR 8034 Israel Security Supplemental $17B (Roll 152, Apr 20 2024) — one of 37 D crossovers; publicly thanked by CAIR-CA. (Scored TRUE because the rubric flags foreign aid to nations the candidate views as conducting hostilities — DeSaulnier conditions Israel aid on Palestinian-civilian considerations.)

3.note: Not listed on TrackAIPAC candidate roster; consistent with his Israel-aid NAY vote — no documented AIPAC PAC support.

4.note: DoD Waste Reduction Act (HR 2367) directly targets $125B Pentagon waste; substantive defense-contractor accountability work.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-11 (2026 D Candidate · advanced to Nov general · Pelosi-endorsed · sitting SF Supervisor D1)

profile.religion: No documented religious affiliation; immigrant from Hong Kong (1991, age 13). Endorsed by Rev. Norman Fong (Chinatown faith leader).
profile.background: SF Board of Supervisors District 1 (since Jan 2021; reelected Nov 2024 with 51.90% RCV). Chairs Budget & Finance Committee and Budget & Appropriations Committee. Prior aide to Sups Sophie Maxwell + Aaron Peskin; aide to DA Kamala Harris; spokesperson SF Rec & Parks + City College SF. Born Chan Sze-man, Hong Kong 1978-10-08.

connie-chan-ca-11.notes_set: SF Supervisor D1 (D, since 2021); 2026 CA-11 D candidate, Pelosi-endorsed, advanced to Nov general election w/ Wiener (June 2 2026: ~28.7%). [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Standard SF progressive on LIFE / MARRIAGE / GENDER / SANCTUARY / ISRAEL. HARD HETERODOX-D CATCHES — VALUABLE: (1) Co-authored Ordinance 206-21 (Oct 2021) making NON-CITIZEN VOTING in SF school-board races permanent — direct election_integrity FALSE that most D candidates don't have a documented vote on; (2) Explicit AIPAC-refusal pledge bundled w/ NRA, pharma, PG&E, fossil-fuel, tobacco PAC refusal (foreign_policy_restraint q3 TRUE); (3) Pledges 'immediate sponsor of the Block the Bombs Act' / arms embargo on Israel + voted AYE on SF BoS Gaza ceasefire resolution Jan 2024 (foreign_policy_restraint q2 TRUE — fits rubric of restraint on foreign military aid); (4) MIXED on police — opposed 2022 Boudin recall + 2023 Breed SFPD overtime supplemental + RESET sobering center, BUT voted AYE on every SFPD budget 2023+ including $160M for recruitment/foot patrols (genuine evolution); (5) Anti-YIMBY housing posture (blocked 495-unit BART development; sought to gut 2025 upzoning amendments). All LGBTQ, abortion, sanctuary, gender-affirming care positions cited from her own campaign issues pages.

0.note: Campaign site: 'Connie will always fight for access to the full range of reproductive health care including abortion'; pledges 'restore full funding for Planned Parenthood.'

1.note: Pledges to 'restore our fundamental right to make decisions about our own bodies and when and if to have children' — opposes any abortion-abolition framework.

0.note: Campaign LGBTQ issues page commits to prohibiting discrimination by sexual orientation and protecting same-sex marriage as a 'fundamental right.'

1.note: Affirms same-sex marriage as a protected right.

2.note: Pledges to 'lift any prohibitions for people on Medicare, Medicaid or other federal health programs to receive gender affirming care'; protects 'right of transgender youth to participate in sports teams consistent with their gender identity'; opposes ban on military trans service.

4.note: Pledges to ban conversion therapy nationally; supports No Place for LGBTQ+ Hate Act — actively promotes LGBTQ identity in federal policy.

1.note: Supports federal protection of 'gender-affirming care' for minors — opposes parental-consent restrictions on those interventions.

0.note: Campaign pledges refusal of NRA PAC money (paired w/ AIPAC, fossil-fuel refusals); endorsed by Moms Demand Action — signals gun-control alignment.

1.note: Endorsed by Moms Demand Action (gun-control group) — HARD evidence on gun-control alignment.

0.note: Campaign site condemns ICE raids that 'terrorize our immigrant communities' and demands end to ICE/CBP 'abuses'; no support for border wall or military border presence.

1.note: Pledges 'pathway to citizenship' for illegal aliens; opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Explicit pledge: 'protecting San Francisco as a sanctuary city' — opposes federal preemption against sanctuary policy.

4.note: Pledges 'protecting and reaffirming birthright citizenship' — explicitly opposes the conservative rubric position.

1.note: HARD HETERODOX-D EVIDENCE: Chan CO-AUTHORED (with Sup Melgar) SF Ordinance 206-21, passed Oct 2021, making NON-CITIZEN VOTING in SF school-board elections permanent — direct vote AGAINST citizenship-verification voter ID.

2.note: HETERODOX-D: Pledges to be 'immediate sponsor of the Block the Bombs Act' and arms embargo on Israel; voted AYE on SF BoS 8-3 Gaza ceasefire resolution Jan 9 2024 — opposes US military aid to nation she characterizes as conducting hostilities. (Christian-conservative rubric note: position derived from anti-Israel framing, not from Israel-as-Christian-ally lens, so flag in profile.)

3.note: Explicit no-AIPAC pledge on campaign site: 'will not take money from corporate PACs, AIPAC or its lobbyists and representatives, the NRA or pharma, PG&E, fossil fuel or tobacco companies' — never accepts AIPAC.

4.note: No documented Pentagon-audit / defense-contractor accountability position; null.

profile.background: Filed FEC C00901462 'Cole Bettles for Congress' Apr 3, 2025 (treasurer = self; address 345 California St, SF). Withdrew before CA-11 primary; did not appear on the June 2, 2026 ballot. No biographical, professional, or policy footprint found. Campaign domain colebettles.com belongs to an unrelated SF singer-songwriter.

cole-bettles.notes_set: [2026-06-04 national-autopilot currency check — STATUS FIX] Cole Bettles WITHDREW from the 2026 CA-11 Democratic primary before ballot certification (Ballotpedia categorizes him 'U.S. House candidate (Withdrew), 2026'; listed in 'Withdrawn or disqualified candidates' on the CA-11 race page; CA SoS official results show 11 candidates and Bettles is not among them). Raised $0; FEC committee C00901462 still technically active w/ no financial activity. NO POLICY POSITIONS FOUND on any rubric category — no campaign site, no social-media presence, no Vote411/Ballotpedia survey, no endorsements, no press coverage. Status flipped to lost + candidacy_status not_running. The status fix IS the win — this record will no longer pollute the active CA-11 D pool.

profile.religion: Catholic — practicing member, St. Dominic's Church parish, San Francisco
profile.background: NYU Law; 25-year SF fintech/crypto lawyer; founder FISC Ventures + 'The Fintech Lawyer.' US Army Reserve combat engineer (veteran). Relocated from Cleveland, OH to SF ~25 years ago. Filed FEC H6CA11276 / committee C00928713 'Daniel Wheeler for Congress Committee' Nov 26, 2025.

daniel-wheeler-ca-11.notes_set: [2026-06-04 national-autopilot currency check — STATUS FIX] Daniel Wheeler WITHDREW from the 2026 CA-11 Democratic primary before ballot certification (not among the 11 candidates on CA SoS official June 2 2026 results; OpenCampaign lists him under 'Withdrawn/Declined Candidates'). Catholic SF fintech lawyer who announced Jan 12 2026; never gained traction. Status flipped to lost + candidacy_status not_running. Limited cited positions on file from his Jan 2026 Bay Area Reporter interview: pro-abortion-rights, pro-same-sex-marriage, called Israel's Gaza campaign 'genocide.' Catholic identity ≠ policy position.

0.note: Bay Area Reporter Jan 2026: described as 'more progressive than the Roman Catholic Church on certain issues, such as ... abortion services' — pro-abortion-rights position despite Catholic affiliation.
Same Bay Area Reporter interview: 'more progressive than the Roman Catholic Church on ... same-sex marriage' — pro-SSM.

2.note: Bay Area Reporter Jan 2026: 'When asked if he felt Israel's killing hundreds of thousands of Palestinians is genocide, Wheeler said he does even though it has not been declared as such by the United Nations' — opposes US aid to Israel under this framing. (As with Chan, scored per the rubric of restraint-on-foreign-aid rather than as biblically pro-Israel.)

profile.religion: No documented religious affiliation (no mention of faith on campaign site or in Ballotpedia survey)
profile.background: Birth name Darren Wayne Helton (per FEC). Las Vegas, NV-born; foster-care youth; Golden Sierra HS; BA Cal State Chico (2012). SF tech career (engineer); fourth-generation Californian. Filed FEC H6CA11243 'Helton for Congress 2026' (C00903682) April 28, 2025 — self-funded w/ $5,247.31 loan, $0 cash on hand.

darren-helton.notes_set: [2026-06-04 national-autopilot currency check — STATUS FIX] Darren Helton WITHDREW from the 2026 CA-11 Democratic primary before ballot certification (Ballotpedia lists him under 'Withdrawn or disqualified candidates'; contribution-history row reads 'Withdrew primary'; did not appear on CA SoS June 2 2026 ballot of 11 candidates). Self-funded ($5,247), zero outside contributions, no endorsements. Status flipped to lost + candidacy_status not_running. NO POLICY POSITIONS FOUND on substantive rubric cells — campaign site/Ballotpedia survey emphasize left-populist class-war framing ('billionaire class gained a trillion dollars while we argued about pronouns and vaccines'), foster-care biography, climate-collapse / parallel-systems advocacy, but no specific stances on life, marriage, 2A, immigration, Israel, or election integrity. (Note: name spelled 'Darren' per FEC, NOT 'Daren.')
